refnamediv.refpurpose: Return a new geometry with coordinates transformed to a different spatial reference system using the inverse of a defined coordinate transformation pipeline.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Return a new geometry with coordinates transformed to a different spatial reference system using a defined coordinate transformation pipeline to go in the inverse direction.
Refer to for details on writing a transformation pipeline.

The SRID of the input geometry is ignored, and the SRID of the output geometry will be set to zero unless a value is provided via the optional to_srid parameter. When using the pipeline is executed in a forward direction. Using `ST_InverseTransformPipeline()` the pipeline is executed in the inverse direction.
Transforms using pipelines are a specialised version of . In most cases `ST_Transform` will choose the correct operations to convert between coordinate systems, and should be preferred.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: Change WGS 84 long lat to UTM 31N using the EPSG:16031 conversion
refsection.programlisting:
-- Inverse direction SELECT ST_AsText(ST_InverseTransformPipeline('POINT(426857.9877165967 5427937.523342293)'::geometry, 'urn:ogc:def:coordinateOperation:EPSG::16031')) AS wgs_geom;
wgs_geom ---------------------------- POINT(2 48.99999999999999) (1 row)

-- using ST_Transform with automatic selection of a conversion pipeline. SELECT ST_AsText(ST_Transform('SRID=4939;POINT(143.0 -37.0)'::geometry, 7844)) AS gda2020_auto;
gda2020_auto ----------------------------------------------- POINT(143.00000635638918 -36.999986706128176) (1 row)
